overview.about: Machu Picchu, the 15th-century Inca citadel set at 2,430m in the Peruvian Andes, was built around 1450 as the royal estate of Inca emperor Pachacuti. It was abandoned during the Spanish conquest and remained unknown to the outside world until American historian Hiram Bingham rediscovered it in 1911.

highlights.label: Inca Royal Citadel — 1450 AD
highlights.desc: Built by emperor Pachacuti at 2,430m — abandoned during the Spanish conquest and hidden for 350 years

highlights.label: Cloud Forest Elevation
highlights.desc: Nestled between dramatic Andean peaks — morning mist rolling across the citadel as the sun rises over Wayna Picchu

highlights.label: Legendary 4-Day Inca Trail
highlights.desc: 43km through cloud forest, high Andean passes and ancient ruins — one of the world's most celebrated trekking routes

highlights.label: Sun Gate — Inti Punku Sunrise
highlights.desc: Reaching the Sun Gate at dawn and seeing Machu Picchu emerge below through the mist is the ultimate trail reward

overview.about: Sacred Valley in Cusco, PE reveals the majesty of Andean civilisation at its peak. Peru's extraordinary heritage landmark heritage blends breathtaking high-altitude landscapes with the archaeological legacy of the Inca Empire — an empire that built wonders in stone with no metal tools, no wheels and no written language.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today

overview.about: Northern Aguas Calientes Financial District in Aguas Calientes, PE reveals the majesty of Andean civilisation at its peak. Peru's extraordinary city destination heritage blends breathtaking high-altitude landscapes with the archaeological legacy of the Inca Empire — an empire that built wonders in stone with no metal tools, no wheels and no written language.

highlights.label: Iconic Skyline & Architecture
highlights.desc: A skyline recognised across the globe — towers, bridges and boulevards that define an entire era of human design

highlights.label: World-Class Arts & Culture
highlights.desc: World-class museums, galleries, theatres and street art that shape global trends in fashion, film and music

highlights.label: Legendary Cuisine & Markets
highlights.desc: A melting pot of culinary traditions — street food, fine dining and bustling markets all within easy reach

highlights.label: Vibrant Nightlife & Events
highlights.desc: Rooftop bars, jazz clubs, night markets and international festivals keep the city electric long after dark

overview.about: Old Aguas Calientes Beach in Aguas Calientes, PE reveals the majesty of Andean civilisation at its peak. highlights.desc: Snorkelling, scuba diving, surfing and sailing in ideal conditions — equipment and instructors available year-round

highlights.label: Exceptional Sunset Views
highlights.desc: Catch the sky blaze in vivid gold and pink as the sun sets over the horizon — cited as a life-changing moment

highlights.label: Protected Marine Ecosystem
highlights.desc: Vibrant coral reefs, sea turtles and tropical fish — responsible tourism preserves these habitats for generations

overview.about: Royal Puno Canyon in Puno, PE reveals the majesty of Andean civilisation at its peak. Peru's extraordinary destination heritage blends breathtaking high-altitude landscapes with the archaeological legacy of the Inca Empire — an empire that built wonders in stone with no metal tools, no wheels and no written language.
